architecture_fit.note: Single attention head = base_model.py softmax with one change (Bayes-optimal inverse temperature); minimal delta, no MLP, perfectly solves the SCC task.

baseline_comparison.note: Vanilla 1/sqrt(d) head and 1/K chance line shown failing on the same head/data, plus a temperature sweep tracing chance->perfect.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic (no trained model); explicitly stated and paired with circuit knock-out ablations (exp/temp/query) and a proposed activation-patching test — strong for a hand-built attempt but no real-model causal check.

operating_range.note: Holds at 1.0 across rho 0.25->4.0 (K=16->256) and the temperature axis spans ~4 orders, but the SCC stays saturated so no in-range break point is exhibited.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: The mechanism is fully hand-set — beta derived analytically from the noise model, not learned — and reproduces the target behaviour exactly.

visual_judgement.note: SCC curve makes the works-vs-fails contrast legible at a glance; temperature sweep, ablation bars and logit-gap panel each add a distinct, purposeful view.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties each chart to the goal: target attention vs rho on the axes, the right baseline overlaid, temperature as the load-bearing axis.

notes: A clean, minimal hand-built attempt: vanilla attention with a Bayes-optimal inverse temperature derived from the generative noise model achieves perfect target attention (scc_auc=1.0) across the whole rho sweep while the vanilla 1/sqrt(d) head collapses to chance. The discovery — temperature, not geometry, is the capacity bottleneck — is well-motivated, supported by circuit ablations, a temperature sweep and a logit-gap argument. Main limits: no real trained-model causal evidence (synthetic by design, acknowledged) and the saturated curve never exhibits an in-range break point.
